evidence.quote_or_summary: Moderate Difficult

values: Suitable for Experienced Trekkers

evidence.quote_or_summary: Suitable for Experienced Trekkers

values: Uneven trails with gradual ascents and descents
Boulder sections with scree and moraine
2.5 km long boulder section before Chandrabhaga Glaciers Viewpoint
Moraine ridges near glacial lakes Kya Tso and Ga Tso
Multiple stream crossings with glacial streams that can swell abruptly later in the day
Open landscape with no vegetation cover at 16,000 ft

evidence.quote_or_summary: every day will include gradual ascents and descents on uneven trails, boulder sections with scree and moraine
There is a 2.5 km long boulder section that you need to traverse before reaching the Chandrabhaga Glaciers Viewpoint. This can be hard on the legs.
While trekking to the high altitude glacial lakes of Kya Tso and Ga Tso, you'll need to climb over moraine ridges.
There are many stream crossings that you need to negotiate on this trek. These glacial streams can swell abruptly in the later parts of the day.
At 16,000 ft above sea-level, there is no vegetation cover and you'll be trekking continuously in open landscape.

values: Climbing over moraine ridges at 16,800 ft is described as strenuous
2.5 km boulder section described as hard on the legs
Daily gradual ascents and descents on uneven trails

evidence.quote_or_summary: At 16,800 ft above sea-level, this can be a strenuous ask.
There is a 2.5 km long boulder section that you need to traverse before reaching the Chandrabhaga Glaciers Viewpoint. This can be hard on the legs.
every day will include gradual ascents and descents on uneven trails

values: Micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes provided by Indiahikes where required
Wind cheaters recommended for wind chill near glaciers

evidence.quote_or_summary: our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc. wherever required.
Layer up well and carry wind cheaters to beat this wind chill.

values: Trek starts at 16,000 ft and climbs to 17,200 ft, described as the highest on any Indiahikes trek
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) especially in the first couple of days
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike; fitness does not eliminate risk if ascent is too fast
Indiahikes team carries high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers

evidence.quote_or_summary: The Chandrabhaga Glaciers Trek begins at a high altitude of 16,000 ft, and climbs further to 17,200 ft. This is the highest we go on any trek in India.
Starting this high and ascending even more puts you at risk of altitude sickness, especially in the first couple of days.

Altitude sickness can happen to anybody - first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers. Fitness helps in making your trek easier. But do not forget that even a fit person climbing too high, too soon runs an equal risk of developing AMS.
The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.

values: Heat exhaustion risk from direct sun exposure in open landscape at 16,000 ft
Strong winds near glaciers cause bitter cold and wind chill
Rain makes boulder sections slippery; Trek Leader may skip sections
Rain often accompanied by hail; seek shelter at nearest campsite
Prolonged rain can flood campsites or make trails slushy, possibly requiring premature return
Indiahikes technical team and Trek Leader monitor weather and may turn back from any campsite if conditions deteriorate

evidence.quote_or_summary: On a sunny day, the direct exposure to harsh sunlight makes you susceptible to Heat Exhaustion.
On a windy day, the strong winds will make you feel bitterly cold. These strong winds are especially possible near the glaciers.
Rains can make the boulder sections slippery and unsafe to navigate. Your Trek Leader may take a call on avoiding such sections altogether in such cases.
If you face rain, it will likely come with hails. Please be prepared to find shelter in such cases as getting hit by hails can be painful.
Prolonged rainfall can flood campsites or make the trails too slushy to walk safely on. You may have to return prematurely from the trek in such cases.
Our technical team, in conjunction with the Trek Leader, constantly monitors the weather conditions and makes informed decisions about proceeding with or aborting the trek.

values: Limited exit points; emergency requires retracing to Baralacha La roadhead
AMS cases must drive immediately toward Manali since Darcha is above 10,000 ft
Closest medical facility: Army Healthcare Center at Zing-Zing Bar
First government healthcare setup at Keylong, ~3 hr drive from Baralacha La
Serious cases require driving back to Manali, 5-6 hours from Baralacha La
Indiahikes team carries high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers
Trekkers must inform Trek Leader of any pre-existing medical conditions

evidence.quote_or_summary: The Chandrabhaga Glaciers Trek has limited exit points. In case of an emergency, you need to retrace your way back to Baralacha La roadhead.
If it is an AMS case, you need to drive immediately towards Manali since even Darcha is at an altitude of above 10,000 ft.
The Army Healthcare Center at Zing-Zing Bar is the closest medical facility in case of an emergency.
The first Government Healthcare Set-up is at Keylong, a 3 hr drive from Baralacha La.
in case of more serious requirements, the only option is to drive back to Manali, which can take anywhere between 5-6 hours from Baralacha La.
The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.
Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns, no matter how minor they may seem.

values: Trekkers must actively participate in safety protocols
Trekkers should disclose pre-existing medical conditions to Trek Leader
Trek Leader may decide to avoid or abort sections/campsites based on weather

evidence.quote_or_summary: we believe that safety is a shared responsibility. We need your active participation and attention to safety protocols to ensure a safe and successful trek.
Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns, no matter how minor they may seem.
If the weather deteriorates, the team may need to turn back from any campsite to ensure the safety of all trekkers.
